channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Let us pay attention, by Bisi Adeleye-Fayemi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: When I was young, there was a family who lived close to us on our street in the Fadeyi area of Lagos. I will call the man of the family Mr Benson. He had a wife, Mrs Remi Benson. When they first arrived on our street, Mr Benson had a motorbike, and his wife would ride […] The post Let us pay

item.title: Sen Kalu distributes educational materials to schools in Abia North
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Senator representing Abia North District at the upper chamber of the National Assembly, Dr. Orji Uzor Kalu, has distributed educational materials to schools across Ohafia Local Government Area of Abia State. The post Sen Kalu distributes educational materials to schools in Abia North appeared fi

item.title: Did ball hit spidercam before England goal? Snicko says not - Norway unsure
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Norway's World Cup campaign ends in a quarter-final defeat by England, but they leave frustrated, arguing that Jude Bellingham's equaliser should not have counted.

item.title: Senate probes alleged NDDC remittance default by oil firm
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Senate has commenced an investigation into the alleged failure of some International Oil Companies (IOCs) to remit statutory contributions to the Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC), with lawmakers giving Aiteo Exploration and Production Company Limited, now known as Nembe Exploration and

item.title: At least two killed in Toronto street festival shooting
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Police have urged the public to stay away from the area.

item.title: Oyo shoolchildren: Blow-by-blow account of how they regained freedom
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Fresh facts have emerged, revealing the blow-by-blow account of the operation that led to the freedom of the 44 schoolchildren abducted in Oyo State. The post Oyo shoolchildren: Blow-by-blow account of how they regained freedom appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: No fool like an old fool!
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Bunmi Sofola ONE bitter pill we’ve all had to swallow is the fact that 419 operators are not only here to stay, their success within Nigeria and abroad has become a nightmare. A few months ago, Jade, a friend, called looking very sorry for herself. A fairly successful investor, she’s a celebrit

item.title: ABIS Group deplores state of abattoirs, floats digital market place
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: ABIS Group has said that the state of abattoirs in Nigeria remains a major concern as many are outdated, poorly maintained, and operate under unhygienic conditions posing a serious challenge to food safety and the livestock industry. The post ABIS Group deplores state of abattoirs, floats digital ma

item.title: Fake agency: What does our senate know? By Tonnie Iredia
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria and its officials led by President Bola Ahmed Tinubu as well as the Oyo State government and its relevant team led Governor Seyi Makinde deserve huge commendations from all Nigerians including this columnist for bringing to an end, the trauma of the abduction of pup

item.title: Perfection is of the Lord, by Funmi Komolafe
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Beloved, the month of July commonly called the month of perfection is progressing and many of us are looking up to God to perfect issues in our lives. Other preachers call the month of July, the month of completeness. The month in which whatever has been shaking in the last six months become stable.

item.title: Swine fever kills 1,000 pigs in Plateau
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Pig farmers in Plateau State have shut down hundreds of farms following an outbreak of African Swine Fever (ASF), with more than 1,000 pigs reportedly killed by the disease. The post Swine fever kills 1,000 pigs in Plateau appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: From maigadi to megida in one administration, by Dele Sobowale
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: “Those who make peaceful revolution impossible will make violent revolution inevitable” – John F Kennedy, US President, 1917-1963 If you don’t know that Nigeria is already involved in a violent revolution, then get ready to read the truth and tremble. Nigerian intellectuals have always asserte

item.title: FREED OYO SCHOOLCHILDREN: Why surivors ‘won’t be handed over’ to Makinde until after 48 hrs
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Presidency, yesterday, disclosed that the schoolchildren and teachers rescued from Oriire community in Ogbomoso, Oyo State, will not be handed over to Governor Seyi Makinde of Oyo State until after 48 hours because they are undergoing medical examinations. The post FREED OYO SCHOOLCHILDREN: Why

item.title: Nigeria, Cameroon, Côte d’Ivoire, Ghana join forces to boost Cocoa production
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria, Cameroon, Côte d’Ivoire, and Ghana have announced plans to join forces to boost Cocoa production. The post Nigeria, Cameroon, Côte d’Ivoire, Ghana join forces to boost Cocoa production appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Gov Okpebholo, observers hail peaceful Edo LG poll despite low turnout, delayed voting
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Edo State Governor, Senator Monday Okpebholo, on Saturday described voter turnout in the state’s local government election as encouraging, even as low voter participation and the late arrival of electoral officials and voting materials affected the exercise in parts of the state The post Gov Okpebho

item.title: Ogun 2027: Late Awujale endorsed Adeola as preferred successor to Abiodun – Orimolusi
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Orimolusi of Ijebu Igbo, Oba Lawrence Jaiyeoba, has disclosed that the late Awujale and Paramount Ruler of Ijebuland, Oba Sikiru Kayode Adetona, while alive, had endorsed the governorship candidate of the All Progressives Congress (APC) Senator Solomon Olamilekan Adeola as his preferred candidat

item.title: Nyityo: Ortom, MUT condemn varsity lecturer’s murder, demand justice
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The immediate past governor of Benue State, Chief Samuel Ortom, has expressed deep shock and sadness over the gruesome killing of renowned academic and Professor of History at Moses Orshio Adasu University, Makurdi (MOAUM), Professor Gabriel Nyityo, describing the incident as a tragic and unacceptab

item.title: Marwa seeks partnership with traditional rulers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA), Brig. Gen. Mohamed Marwa (Rtd) has called for greater protection of the girl-child against systemic vulnerabilities that push women and youth toward drug abuse and exploitation. The post Marwa seeks partner

item.title: Unsung Patron: How Demola Aladekomo helped reawaken Nigerian theatre
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: AKINTAYO ABODUNRIN looks back at Sir Aladekomo’s overlooked role in reviving Nigerian theatre through the Chams Theatre Series, and the nation’s failure to preserve cultural history The post Unsung Patron: How Demola Aladekomo helped reawaken Nigerian theatre appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Speaker of the House, Rt. Hon. Abbas Tajudeen has lauded the re-nomination of Vice President Kashim Shettima as the All Progressives Congress (APC) vice presidential candidate for the 2027 general election. item.title: Nigeria rescues dozens of abducted school children in Oyo state
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Nigerian security forces have rescued 39 schoolchildren and five teachers abducted nearly two months ago in Oyo state.

item.description: The strikes follow an attack on a Cyrpus-flagged vessel travelling through the critical waterway.

item.title: China's second typhoon in a week makes landfall
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Nearly two million people are evacuated from eastern Zhejiang province, with the city of Wenzhou close to the path of the storm.

item.description: The Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG) has described the rescue of pupils and teachers abducted from schools in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State as the fulfilment of a divine promise. item.title: Argentina-Switzerland live: Defending champions take lead through Mac Allister's early opener
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Argentina and Switzerland clash in a quarter-final encounter at the World Cup, with both nations seeking to take on England in the semi-finals. The Albiceleste aim to extend their title defence, while Nati look to continue their historic tournament run after ending a decades-long knockout curse.

item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Disturbing reports of mob attacks have been coming out of Kaduna State lately. First was the tragic case of the Islamic teacher, Mallama Ummulkhairi Usman Aliyu, who was brutally attacked and lynched in broad daylight by a lawless mob. According to an eyewitness account, Mallama was travelling to at

item.title: Mediators push US-Iran talks in Qatar and Oman to avert escalation
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Regional mediators are stepping up efforts to prevent further escalation between Iran and the US.

item.title: World Cup: Bellingham brace sends England to semi-final
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Jude Bellingham scored twice, including an extra-time winner to ensure England secured a 2-1 win over Norway in their 2026 FIFA World Cup quarter-final match at the Miami stadium. The midfielder ensured England overpowered a Norway side that took the lead and had a goal ruled out for offside in the

item.title: Iran shuts Strait of Hormuz amid fresh hostilities with US
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Strait of Hormuz, a major route of petroleum products for Europe, Asia and some parts of the world, has been shut amid renewed hostilities between the United States and Iran. The Strait was initially shut during the war between US, Israel and Iran, shooting up fuel prices across the world. But after

item.title: MSMEs N500m revolving loan scheme
item.dc:creator: The Sun Editorial
item.description: Recently, the Small and Medium Enterprises Development of Nigeria (SMEDAN) unveiled a N500 million revolving, zero-interest loan called “Grow Fund for Businesses” to boost small businesses across the country. The Director-General of SMEDAN, Mr. Charles Odii, announced the initiative recently in Abuj

item.title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: When we last sat down with Jobs at TechCrunch Disrupt nearly three years ago, his firm Yosemite was brand new and biotech was still reeling from its post-pandemic crash. Now, the venture outfit has a team of 17; a cluster of blockbuster drugs are all losing patent protection in roughly the same wind

item.title: Bellingham scores twice as England beat Norway 2-1 to reach World Cup semis
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Jude Bellingham scores in extra-time to send England into the World Cup semifinals for first time since 2018.

item.title: Bellingham Bags Brace, Equals Maradona’s World Cup Feat As England Overcome Norway To Reach Semifinals
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Jude Bellingham sent England into the World Cup semifinals with two goals as the Three Lions overturned a 1-0 deficit to seal a 2-1 extra-time win and end Norway hopes of a first-ever last four appearance in the tournament, ESPN reports. Bellingham became the first player to score back-to-back doubl

item.title: Who else? How Bellingham became indispensable again
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Senior football correspondent Sami Mokbel charts Jude Bellingham's remarkable resurgence with England at the World Cup.

item.title: World Cup: Bellingham Sends England Into Semifinals With Extra-time Heroics
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Jude Bellingham was the hero once again as England’s Three Lions clawed their way back from a goal down to defeat Norway 2-1 in a thrilling World Cup Quarter-Final clash, securing their spot in the semi-finals. Norway stunned England in the 36th minute when A. Schjelderup, assisted by Martin Ø

item.title: My goal is to mentor more young chefs, elevate culinary education — Chef Giggles
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: For more than a decade, Chef Rukayat Momoh, popularly known as Chef Giggles, has steadily transformed Nigeria’s culinary landscape, building a reputation that extends far beyond the kitchen through innovation, education, mentorship, and entrepreneurship. item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets

item.description: England have had heroes before - but Jude Bellingham is a special one.

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Thomas Tuchel admitted he was not happy with England's performance in their nervy World Cup quarter-final win over Norway - admitting the Three Lions were "sloppy, lucky" and not good enough in "every sense".

item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: A 67 million-year-old Tyrannosaurus rex will go on sale in New York with a pre-sale value of $30m.

item.title: W/Cup: England Overcome Norway In Extra Time, Advance To Semifinals
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: England booked their place in the semifinals of the FIFA World Cup after defeating Norway 2-1 in a dramatic extra-time contest on Saturday night, with Jude Bellingham scoring twice to inspire Thomas Tuchel’s side to victory. Bellingham struck in first-half stoppage time and again moments into

item.title: Bellingham breaks Norway’s dream, powers England into World Cup last four
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: With the match heading towards a penalty shootout, Bellingham once again proved decisive. In the 93rd minute of extra time, the Real Madrid midfielder reacted quickest to a rebound inside the penalty area, rifling the ball into the net for his second goal of the night and England's winner.

item.title: England beat Norway after extra-time to reach World Cup semi-final
item.dc:creator: Ripples Nigeria
item.description: England have advanced to the semifinals of the 2026 World Cup after beating Norway 2-1 after extra-time in a thrilling quarterfinal clash in Miami on Saturday night. Andreas Schjelderup opened scoring for Norway in the 36th minute as they capped off a superb start against the Three Lions. The Englis

item.description: England's World Cup semi-final against Argentina or Switzerland will be broadcast live on BBC One and BBC iPlayer.

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Jude Bellingham scores his second goal of the game to give England a 2-1 lead over Norway during extra time of their 2026 World Cup quarter-final in Miami.

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England have a penalty overturned following a VAR review after Djed Spence was judged to have initiated the contact with Norway's Oscar Bobb during the first half of extra time in Miami.

item.description: Pieces from the turf used at the stadium will go on sale as memorabilia and will be priced from $450 to $1,200.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: In a tense, rain-soaked final at the Stephen Keshi Stadium in Asaba on Saturday, El-Kanemi Warriors etched their name deeper into Nigerian football history, clinching their fourth President Federation Cup crown with a gritty 1-0 triumph over gallant Ikorodu City. The solitary strike, a coolly conver

item.title: In Trinidad and Tobago, Where Africa and India Meet
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The community pooled money to purchase the freedom of other Muslims and reportedly numbered about 140 by the 1830s. Bath unsuccessfully petitioned the British authorities to repatriate the community to Africa.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The United Kingdom has updated its Immigration Rules Appendix Visitor, reaffirming the list of countries and categories of travellers required to obtain a visa before travelling to the UK for visits or any stay of less than six months. The updated guidance, released by the UK Home Office on July 1 a

item.dc:creator: Leadership

The campaign began in Makurdi, Benue State, with a sensitisation progr

item.title: Fifa official refuses to answer BBC's Balogun ban questions
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: The head of Fifa's disciplinary committee refuses to answer BBC questions about the decision-making process which led to United States striker Folarin Balogun avoiding a ban after being sent off.

item.title: Zamfara Partners Security Agencies To Enforce Child Protection Law
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Zamfara State Government has reaffirmed its commitment to working closely with security agencies and other stakeholders to strengthen child protection and ensure the strict enforcement of the Zamfara State Child Protection Law, 2022, against anyone found violating the rights of children.

item.title: Argentina midfield great Rattin dies aged 89
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Argentina great Antonio Rattin, whose sending-off at the 1966 World Cup led to the introduction of red and yellow cards, dies aged 89.

item.title: Federation Cup Final: El-Kanemi outclass Ikorodu City to claim fourth title
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: It was a disappointing end to the season for Ikorodu City. The Lagos club not only lost the cup final but had also missed the chance to qualify for continental football on the final day of the Nigeria Premier Football League season.

item.title: ₦228bn: The Day The North Decided To Buy Back Its Peace

item.description: …For years the North spoke in resolutions. On Monday in Kaduna, it spoke in naira. item.description: Italian football icon Paolo Maldini has been appointed technical director of the national football federation (FIGC) looking to rebuild after Italy's failure to qualify for a third consecutive World Cup. item.title: Bulldozers move in as Sanwo-Olu declares war on illegal highway markets, shanties
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The governor said the clearance operation aligns with the state's plan to transform the Lagos-Badagry Expressway into a modern 10-lane highway capable of supporting increased traffic and improving transportation across the metropolis.

item.title: Obi To Umahi: Become Presidential Candidate Before Challenging Me To Debate
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, has dismissed Works Minister David Umahi’s call for a public debate, saying the minister must first become a presidential candidate before seeking such an engagement.   Umahi had challenged Obi to a

item.title: This slushie machine was a lifesaver during NYC’s heat wave
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Last weekend’s brutal NYC heat wave had me craving a frozen drink almost every afternoon. Normally, that would mean sweating through a walk to 7-Eleven for a slurpee. This time, though, I stayed home and put the new Ninja Slushi Twist to the test. Ninja’s latest slushie machine builds on

item.title: RISCBoy is an open-source portable games console, designed from scratch
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/Wren6991/RISCBoy Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48876245 Points: 60 # Comments: 16

item.title: Abducted Benue Pastor Dies After Regaining Freedom From Captivity
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The abducted pastor of the Nongo u Kristu u i Ser u sha Tar (NKST) Church, Rev. Samuel Gbinde, has died shortly after regaining freedom from captivity in Benue State. Rev. Gbinde and two members of his congregation were abducted on June 30, 2026, when armed men invaded his residence at the NKST Chur

item.title: Polish PM pledges memorial to victims of WW2 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Warsaw and Kyiv have for decades been at loggerheads over the killings of many thousands of civilians during World War Two.

item.title: US judge dismisses January 6 case against Proud Boys after Trump order
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The case is the latest instance of the Trump administration seeking to unravel prosecutions against January 6 rioters.

item.title: Nasarawa Amazons Win 2026 Women’s President Federation Cup
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nasarawa Amazons FC have emerged champions of the 2026 Women’s President Federation Cup after defeating Heartland Queens FC of Owerri The victory came through penalty shootout at the Stephen Keshi Stadium in Asaba. The keenly contested final ended 1-1 after regulation time, with Nasarawa Amazo
